taxonomy: Majana richardiana is classified as a species within the genus Majana, which is part of the family Poaceae. The exact classification of Majana richardiana may vary depending on the source, but it is generally recognized as a type of grass. Further classification may include the order and class of the plant, which would be Poales and Liliopsida, respectively. However, without more specific information, the complete classification of Majana richardiana may not be readily available. More research is necessary to determine the exact classification of this species.
uses: Majana richardiana is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. It is also employed to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and indigestion. In some cultures, it is used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits.

side_effects: Majana richardiana, also known as the Mexican prickly poppy, may cause skin irritation and allergic reactions due to its sap or spines, which can cause inflammation and redness.
